rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Transferring Venue Of This Action To Sacramento County
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Campbell Construction Co.'s Motion for an Order Transferring Venue of This Action to Sacramento County: Hearing Required. The Court did not receive courtesy copies of the moving papers pursuant to Local Rule 2.7B. The hearing will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ANDERSEN VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Ameron International Corporation's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED in its entirety. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding Plaintiff's claims for Intentional Tort, False Representation, and Punitive Damages. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 843, 850-51.) Defendant did not provide Plaintiff's responses to its special interrogatories set one. Plaintiff's deposition testimony that he has no personal knowledge of Defendant's conduct is insufficient, in of itself, to shift the burden. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is improper as to medical damages as this is an evidentiary issue to be determined at trial. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ANDERSEN VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Familian Corp's Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding Plaintiff's claims for Intentional Tort, False Representation, and Punitive Damages. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 843, 850-51.) Plaintiff's responses to Defendant's special interrogatories are factually devoid as it contains boilerplate answers that restate Plaintiff's allegations and laundry lists of people and/or documents. (Defendant's Index of Exhibits, Ex. C [Plaintiff's Second Amended Responses to Familian Corp.'s Special Interrogatories No. 5, 8, and 9 at 6-24; 25-62]; Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal. App. 4th 96, 107.) Plaintiff failed to submit evidence to create a triable issue of fact. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ANDERSEN VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant VWR International LLC's Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant sustained its initial burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) The declarations of Gordon Andersen and Charles Ay create a triable issue of fact as to whether Plaintiff was exposed to asbestos as a result of his use of protective clothing including the gloves, mittens, arm coverings, aprons, and suits supplied by Defendant. Defendant's objections to the declarations of Gordon Andersen and Charles Ay are overruled. As to Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication, it is denied in its entirety for the same reasons stated above. For each summary adjudication issue, Defendant relies on the same set of undisputed material facts used in support of its motion for summary judgment. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ANDERSEN VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Defendant Watts Regulator Co.S Affirmative Defenses
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Adjudication of Defendant Watts Regulator Co.'s Affirmative Defenses is GRANTED. Defendant has withdrawn Defenses Nos. 10, 13, 20, 24, 28, 30, 32, 34, 35, 38-39, 41-43, and 45. (Opp at 2.) Defenses Nos. 1, 6, 9, 14, 18, 22, 23, 25, 27, 29, 33, and 40 are not proper affirmative defenses as they either involve questions of law or are assertions that Plaintiff cannot prove his causes of action. As to all other defenses, Plaintiff sustained its burden of demonstrating that Defendant does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding its affirmative defenses. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 843, 850-51.) Defendant's responses to Plaintiff's special interrogatories are factually devoid as it fails to state specific facts in support of Defendant's claims. (Tashjian Declaration, Ex. D [Defendant Watts Regulator Co.'s Supplemental Response to Plaintiff Gordon Andersen's Special Interrogatories Nos. 30-32 at 18-19]; Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal. App. 4th 96, 107.) Defendant failed to submit evidence to create a triable issue of fact. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
